round2.disagreesWith: Glass Cannon focuses on marginal shooting differences when historically A-10 champs with VCU's profile consistently outperform their seeds regardless of three-point percentage.
round2.agreesWith: Streak correctly identifies VCU's peak timing - historically, teams entering tournaments on 7+ game winning streaks win 68% of first-round games.

round2.name: STREAK
round2.quote: Tempo Hawk's defensive stats are stale! VCU's defense is peaking RIGHT NOW, holding opponents to 60 points per game in their conference tournament. Momentum is everything!
round2.disagreesWith: Tempo Hawk wants to talk about season-long defense, but VCU's recent defensive clampdown is far more relevant, holding teams way below their averages.
round2.agreesWith: Oracle gets it! VCU's perfect recent form and conference tournament championship are exactly what I'm seeing. They're on an emotional high, just like 2011.

upsetWatch.description:
Upset score 49/100 for #11 VCU over #10 Texas A&M. Historical upset rate: 50.0%. Agent support: 5-2 for VCU.
   *Agent split: 5-2 for VCU | Historical upset rate: 50.0%*
   *Statistical edges: None*

conductor.quote: Iron Curtain and Streak won this debate - VCU's elite defense travels and their momentum is peaking at the perfect time. Whisper's flip on travel fatigue was compelling but couldn't overcome the defensive efficiency argument. Glass Cannon and Road Dog lost betting on marginal shooting edges over proven defensive dominance. Defense wins championships.
conductor.keyFactor: VCU's elite defense neutralizing Texas A&M's offensive weapons in a low-possession grinder
conductor.dissentReport: Glass Cannon overvalued marginal three-point shooting differences while Road Dog's experience argument couldn't overcome VCU's superior efficiency metrics and current form
